pattern: Balanced numbers are sparse but densely clustered enough within this constraint's range that simply counting upward from n+1 and checking the 'digit d appears exactly d times' rule against each candidate always converges quickly.
hints: A balanced number can never contain the digit 0, since 0 would have to occur exactly zero times -- meaning it can't appear at all.
For a candidate number, count how many times each digit occurs and check, for every digit that actually appears, whether its count equals the digit's own value.
Increment from n+1 and test each candidate with that digit-count check; within this problem's bound on n, a balanced number is never far away.

edge_inputs.description: just below the first balanced number above 20
n near the top of the allowed range
n at the maximum allowed value
n is itself a balanced number, so the next one must be strictly greater
n is a larger balanced number, must not return itself
a mid-range value with no balanced number nearby

stress.description: random n spread across the full allowed range

complexity.time: O(1) amortized within the problem's bound on n
